title: Stop extracting data: Run serverless Python natively in BigQuery!
summary: Google Cloud has made Python User Defined Functions (UDFs) generally available in BigQuery, allowing users to run custom Python code natively within SQL statements without infrastructure management. The feature supports importing libraries like BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers for serverless execution.
writeup: This video covers the general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, which run custom Python code directly inside SQL statements without infrastructure management. The point is to process data where it lives rather than extracting it to a separate environment. Concrete takeaways: UDFs execute serverlessly, and they can import libraries such as BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers for tasks like inference within a query.
